questions.text: The sexual division of labour refers to a system in which:
options: Both men and women do all jobs
All work inside the home is done by women or organized by them
Men are responsible for all household chores
Jobs are assigned based on educational qualifications only
questions.explanation: Sexual division of labour is a system where domestic work is primarily expected to be handled by women, while men work outside.

questions.text: Radical women's movements aiming at equality in personal and family life as well are called:
questions.explanation: Feminist movements are those that advocate for political, social, and economic equality for women.

questions.text: In which of these countries is the participation of women in public life very high?
questions.explanation: Scandinavian countries like Sweden, Norway, and Finland have very high levels of women's participation in public life.

questions.text: According to the textbook, what is the approximate literacy rate among women in India?
questions.explanation: While it changes over time, the NCERT textbook mentions women's literacy at around 54% compared to 76% for men.

questions.text: The Equal Remuneration Act of 1976 provides that:
options: Men should be paid more than women
Equal wages should be paid for equal work
Women should only work in teaching
Only men can work in factories
questions.explanation: This law mandates that for the same work, men and women must be paid the same salary or wages.

questions.text: In India, the proportion of women in the legislature has been:
questions.explanation: Women's representation in the Indian Parliament has remained low, often below 15% in the Lok Sabha.

questions.text: How many seats are reserved for women in local government bodies (Panchayats and Municipalities) in India?
questions.explanation: One-third of seats in local government bodies are reserved for women, bringing over 10 lakh elected women representatives to power.

questions.text: A 'Patriarchal' society is one that is:
options: Equally shared by men and women
questions.explanation: Patriarchy literally means rule by father and refers to a system that values men more and gives them power over women.

questions.text: Communalism refers to a situation where:
options: One religious group is pitted against another
Religion is kept separate from politics
questions.explanation: Communal politics is based on the idea that religion is the principal basis of social community.

questions.text: The most common form of communalism in daily life is:
questions.explanation: Daily communalism often manifests as beliefs in the superiority of one's religion over others.

questions.text: A secular state is one where:
options: The state has its own official religion
The state does not have an official religion and provides freedom to all
questions.explanation: Secularism in India means the Constitution does not give special status to any religion.

questions.text: Who among the following said that religion can never be separated from politics?
questions.explanation: Gandhi believed politics should be guided by ethics and moral values drawn from all religions.

questions.text: Family laws deal with which of the following matters?
questions.explanation: Family laws are those that govern domestic relations like adoption, marriage, and succession.

questions.text: Caste hierarchy is a ladder-like formation in which:
options: All caste groups are placed from the 'highest' to the 'lowest'
questions.explanation: It is a social structure where some castes are considered superior and others inferior.

questions.text: Which of the following social reformers worked for the establishment of a casteless society in India?
questions.explanation: Many leaders advocated for the end of the caste system and the practice of untouchability.

questions.text: What is one major factor that has contributed to the weakening of the caste system in India?
questions.explanation: Socio-economic changes like occupational mobility and education have blurred caste boundaries.

questions.text: When we say 'caste in politics', we mean:
options: Caste groups are used as vote banks by political parties
Caste is the only factor in voting
questions.explanation: Political parties often choose candidates based on the caste composition of the electorate to win elections.

questions.text: Which of the following is TRUE about 'politics in caste'?
options: Caste groups try to become bigger by incorporating neighboring sub-castes
Politics has no impact on caste
questions.explanation: Politics influences caste by bringing various groups together into larger coalitions to gain bargaining power.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a form of communalism?
options: Desire to form a separate political unit
Treating all citizens as equal regardless of religion
questions.explanation: Equality regardless of religion is a feature of secularism, not communalism.

questions.text: The Child Sex Ratio in India has been:
questions.explanation: Due to preference for male children and sex-selective abortion, the ratio of girls to boys has fallen in many parts of India.

questions.text: What was the main demand of the feminist movements?
questions.explanation: Feminists sought equal opportunities in education, career, and political representation.

questions.text: Communal politics is based on the idea that:
options: Religion is the principal basis of social community
Caste is more important than religion
questions.explanation: This ideology believes that followers of one religion must belong to one community with identical interests.

questions.text: In India, the Constitution prohibits discrimination on the grounds of:
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution ensures equality for all citizens by forbidding discrimination on multiple social grounds.

questions.text: Untouchability was officially ________ by the Constitution of India.
questions.explanation: The Constitution banned the practice of untouchability to promote social equality.

questions.text: When a person believes that his or her own religion is superior to that of others, it leads to:
questions.explanation: This mindset is the fundamental root of communal conflict.

questions.text: Occupational mobility means:
options: Moving from one city to another for a job
Shift from one occupation to another, usually across generations
questions.explanation: This shift breaks the traditional link between caste and specific occupations.

questions.text: Urbanisation refers to:
options: Shift of population from towns to villages
Shift of population from rural areas to urban areas
questions.explanation: Urbanisation is a key factor in changing traditional social structures like caste.

questions.text: The belief that all the followers of a religion must have the same interests is a flaw of:
questions.explanation: Communalism fails to recognize that individuals within a religion have different roles and needs.

questions.text: Which of these is a way to increase women's representation in politics?
questions.explanation: Reservation of seats ensures that women have a guaranteed presence in decision-making bodies.

questions.text: The idea of using caste as a basis for political mobilization was expected to:
options: Bring together people of different castes
Help disadvantaged castes gain a say in power
questions.explanation: Caste politics has often allowed lower castes to demand their space and share in power.

questions.text: What is the status of the Women's Reservation Bill in the Indian Parliament?
options: Pending for more than a decade
questions.explanation: While passed in the Rajya Sabha at one point, it has faced hurdles in becoming a law for the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies.

questions.text: Casteism is based on the assumption that:
options: Caste is the only basis of social community
Religion is more important than caste
questions.explanation: Casteism assumes that people belonging to the same caste naturally form a separate social community.

questions.text: Secularism is not just an ideology of some parties, but is the ________ of our Constitution.
questions.explanation: Secularism is a core value that ensures the unity and integrity of India.

questions.text: In a democracy, political expression of social divisions is:
questions.explanation: In a healthy democracy, diverse groups can express their grievances and negotiate for their rights.

questions.text: The 'Scheduled Castes' and 'Scheduled Tribes' are groups that:
options: Faced discrimination and exclusion for centuries
questions.explanation: These groups have historically been marginalized and are now provided constitutional protections.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a reason for the decline of the caste system?
questions.explanation: Old rituals and rigid notions of purity/pollution are declining due to modern influences.

questions.text: Gender division is usually understood as:
questions.explanation: It is a form of social division based on what society expects from men and women.

questions.text: What happens when caste is treated as the only basis of politics?
options: It can divert attention from other pressing issues like poverty
questions.explanation: Exclusive focus on caste can lead to tensions and ignore broader developmental needs.

questions.text: Universal Adult Franchise helped in:
options: Giving every citizen one vote and equal value
questions.explanation: One person, one vote forced political leaders to treat people of all castes as important for winning elections.

questions.text: To counter communalism, the Indian Constitution:
options: Allows the state to intervene in religious matters to ensure equality
questions.explanation: The state can intervene (like banning untouchability) to ensure that religious practices do not violate equality.
